widgets_values: To use audio conditioning, remove the bypass from these nodes and replace the empty latent with the Set Latent Noise Mask.

If you bypass the second pass, it's advisable not to downscale the video. That is, setting this value to 1.0 or 0.5 means you're generating a video with half the specified size. Additionally, you need to adjust the subject that SAM3 will use in the second pass.
To use bong_tangent you need to install the RES4LYF nodes. https://github.com/ClownsharkBatwing/RES4LYF
If your video doesn't have audio, it might cause an error, so it's best to remove the audio from the "create video" field.

nodes.title: Manual Prompt
widgets_values: Remove the car in the background

You are a prompt normalizer for visual editing captions.

Your task is to convert an informal user request into a single caption that matches the style of a visual editing training dataset.

Rules:
- Write in English.
- Return only one sentence.
- Start with exactly one action verb: Add, Remove, Replace, or Convert.
- Be concise, objective, and visually specific.
- Clearly identify the edit target.
- Include concrete visual attributes when useful: color, clothing, material, size, pose, expression.
- Include spatial grounding when possible: on the left, on the right, in the center, in the foreground, on the table, next to, behind.
- Do not explain anything.
- Do not use vague language.
- Use Add for inserting a new visible element.
- Use Remove for deleting an existing visible element.
- Use Replace for swapping or changing one visible element into another.
- Use Convert for style-only transformations.

User request: {USER_REQUEST}

Return only the normalized caption.

nodes.title: Model Links
widgets_values:
# LTX-2.3

* Hugging Face: [Lightricks/LTX-2.3](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/)
* GitHub: [LTX-2](https://github.com/Lightricks/LTX-2)

## LTX-2.3 Prompting Tips

1. **Core Actions**: describe events and actions as they happen over time
2. **Visual Details**: describe all visual details you want to appear in the video
3. **Audio**: describe any sounds and dialogue needed for the scene

## Report LTX-2.3 Issues

To report issues when running this workflow, go here:
[https://github.com/Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo/issues](https://github.com/Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo/issues)

---

## Required Models and Files

### diffusion_models

**Option 1**

* [ltx-2-3-22b-dev_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/diffusion_models/ltx-2-3-22b-dev_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled.safetensors)


* [ltx-2.3-22b-dev-Q6_K.gguf](https://huggingface.co/unsloth/LTX-2.3-GGUF/blob/main/ltx-2.3-22b-dev-Q6_K.gguf)


> This model requires the **distilled LoRA** if you want to generate videos in **8 steps**.

**Option 2**

* [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled_v3.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/diffusion_models/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled_v3.safetensors)

* [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-1.1_transformer_only_fp8_scaled.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/diffusion_models/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-1.1_transformer_only_fp8_scaled.safetensors) - Added 16-04-2026

> This models **does not require** the distilled LoRA.

---

### vaes

* [LTX23_audio_vae_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/vae/LTX23_audio_vae_bf16.safetensors)
* [LTX23_video_vae_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/vae/LTX23_video_vae_bf16.safetensors)

**For preview**

* [taeltx2_3.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/vae/taeltx2_3.safetensors)

---

### projection text encoder

* [ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/text_encoders/ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16.safetensors)

---

### text encoder

You can download the text encoder here:

* [gemma_3_12B_it_fp8_scaled.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/ltx-2/blob/main/split_files/text_encoders/gemma_3_12B_it_fp8_scaled.safetensors)

---

### loras

* [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-1.1_lora-dynamic_fro09_avg_rank_111_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/loras/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-1.1_lora-dynamic_fro09_avg_rank_111_bf16.safetensors)

* [ltx23_edit_anything_global_rank128_v1_7500steps.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Alissonerdx/LTX-LoRAs/blob/main/ltx23_edit_anything_global_rank128_v1_7500steps.safetensors)

* [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-384-1.1.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-384-1.1.safetensors)

> If you download
> **ltx-2-3-22b-dev_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled.safetensors**,
> use the LoRA above.

---

### upscalers

**Spatial upscaler**

* [ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/blob/main/ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors)

**Temporal upscaler**

* [ltx-2.3-temporal-upscaler-x2-1.0.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/blob/main/ltx-2.3-temporal-upscaler-x2-1.0.safetensors)

---

## Model Folder Structure

```text
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 diffusion_models/
│   │   ├── ltx-2-3-22b-dev_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled.safetensors
│   │   └── ltx-2.3-22b-distilled_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled_v3.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 vae/
│   │   ├── LTX23_audio_vae_bf16.safetensors
│   │   ├── LTX23_video_vae_bf16.safetensors
│   │   └── taeltx2_3.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 text_encoders/
│   │   ├── ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16.safetensors
│   │   └── gemma_3_12B_it_fp8_scaled.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 loras/
│   │   ├── ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-dynamic_fro09_avg_rank_105_bf16.safetensors
│   │   ├── ltx23_edit_anything_global_rank128_v1_7500steps.safetensors
│   └── 📂 latent_upscale_models/
│       ├── ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors
│       └── ltx-2.3-temporal-upscaler-x2-1.0.safetensors
```

---

## Important Notes

* If you use **`ltx-2-3-22b-dev_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled.safetensors`**, you will also need the **distilled LoRA** to generate videos in **8 steps**.
* If you use **`ltx-2.3-22b-distilled_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled_v3.safetensors`**, the **LoRA is not required**.
* **`taeltx2_3.safetensors`** is used for **preview**.
* Both **spatial** and **temporal** upscalers are optional.

---

## Report Issues

Before reporting any issue, make sure to update ComfyUI first:
[ComfyUI update guide](https://docs.comfy.org/installation/update_comfyui)

> Note: Desktop and Cloud releases follow stable builds, so some models supported in nightly versions may not be available yet.

### Where to report each issue type

* Cannot run / runtime errors: [ComfyUI/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/ComfyUI/issues)
* UI / frontend issues: [ComfyUI_frontend/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/ComfyUI_frontend/issues)
* Workflow issues: [workflow_templates/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/workflow_templates/issues)
To use audio conditioning, remove the bypass from these nodes and replace the empty latent with the Set Latent Noise Mask.

You are a prompt normalizer for video editing training captions.

Your task is to rewrite an informal user request into a single training-style caption for video editing.

Important:
- The domain is video, not still images.
- The output must describe an edit to a video scene or subject.
- Do not refer to the result as an image, photo, photograph, or picture.
- Do not use wording that implies still-image editing.

Choose exactly one action verb:
- Add
- Remove
- Replace
- Convert

Rules:
- Write exactly one sentence in English.
- Start with exactly one of the four action verbs.
- Preserve the original edit intent.
- Do not merely fix grammar; expand the request into a more detailed and visually specific caption.
- Make the output sound like a dataset caption for video editing.
- Add concrete visual attributes whenever useful: color, size, material, clothing, texture, hairstyle, expression, species, shape, age, or design details.
- Add spatial grounding whenever possible: on the left, on the right, in the center, in the foreground, in the background, on the road, on the table, next to, behind, in front of, or occupying the same position.
- If the request is underspecified, enrich it with generic but visually plausible detail without changing the main intent.
- Prefer outputs that describe visible edits to subjects, objects, clothing, props, backgrounds, overlays, or scene elements in a video.
- Avoid minimal rewrites.
- Do not explain anything.
- Do not output multiple options.
- Do not use conversational language.

Additional constraints:
- Never use the words: image, photo, photograph, picture.
- Prefer words such as: video, scene, frame, subject, person, object, background, foreground, clip.
- Do not mention cameras, quality settings, or generation instructions.

Example:
Input: Replace the blue car by a ship
Output: Replace the blue car with a large white and blue ship occupying the same position in the scene.

Return only the normalized caption.

User request:
Convert the video into a ghibili anime style.

nodes.title: Very Very Important
widgets_values:
# Prompt Patterns by Task

## 1. Add
Structure:
```text
Add a/an [subject/object] with [clear visual attributes], [pose/orientation if relevant], [precise location in the scene].
````

## 2. Remove

Structure:

```text
Remove the [subject/object] [precise location or identifying description].
```

## 3. Replace

Structure:

```text
Replace the [original subject/object] [precise location or identifying description] with a/an [new subject/object] with [clear visual attributes].
```

## 4. Style

Structure:

```text
Convert the video into a [style name] style.
```

---

# Master Template for Improving Prompts

```

You are a prompt normalizer for visual editing training captions.

Your task is to convert an informal user request into a single caption that matches the style of a visual editing training dataset.

Goal:
- Do not merely correct grammar.
- Rewrite the request into a more detailed, visually grounded training-style caption.
- Preserve the original edit intent while making the caption more specific and descriptive.

Task selection:
- Use "Add" when the request inserts a new visible element.
- Use "Remove" when the request deletes an existing visible element.
- Use "Replace" when the request swaps or changes one visible element into another.
- Use "Convert" when the request is purely a style transformation.

Caption rules:
- Write exactly one sentence in English.
- Start with exactly one verb: Add, Remove, Replace, or Convert.
- Keep the original meaning of the user request.
- Make the caption more detailed than the input whenever possible.
- Add concrete visual attributes for the subject or replacement when useful, such as color, size, material, clothing, texture, species, facial features, hairstyle, or shape.
- Add spatial grounding whenever possible, such as on the left, on the right, in the center, in the foreground, in the background, on the table, on the road, next to, behind, in front of, or occupying the same position.
- If the user request is underspecified, enrich the caption in a generic but visually plausible way without changing the main intent.
- Keep the caption concise, but more descriptive than a direct paraphrase.
- Do not explain your reasoning.
- Do not output multiple options.
- Do not use conversational language.

Bad example:
Input: Replace the blue car by a ship
Output: Replace the blue car with a ship.

Better example:
Input: Replace the blue car by a ship
Output: Replace the blue car with a large white and blue ship occupying the same position in the scene.

Return only the normalized caption.

User request:

```
---

# Example

## Input

```text
I want to change the clothing to red.
```

## Output

```text
Replace the shirt on the person with a red shirt.
```

```

nodes.title: Describe the task here (Prompt)
widgets_values: Convert the video into a ghibili anime style.

nodes.title: Final Prompt
widgets_values: Convert the video into a vibrant, hand-drawn anime style with soft lighting and painterly textures, emulating the aesthetic of Studio Ghibli films.
